instructions.content: Run markdownlint locally and fix violations until zero errors. Identify the specific rule, fix the source content or apply a narrow inline exception, then re-lint. Do not proceed until lint is clean.
Use fenced code blocks with explicit language tags (e.g., ```bash, ```ts) instead of bare triple backticks.
Never skip heading levels. Increase heading levels one step at a time (H1 → H2 → H3, not H1 → H3).
Keep list markers consistent throughout a document. Do not mix -, *, and +.
Never suppress markdownlint rules broadly. Fix source content or apply narrow, justified inline exceptions.
Use ATX-style headings (#) with a single space after the hash. Keep one H1 per document.
Structure README with sections: Overview, Installation, Usage, Configuration, API Reference, Contributing, License.
Keep Markdown lint-clean and deterministic. Use consistent file-level conventions across the repository.
Write with active voice, present tense, and address the reader directly. Be concise and specific.
Organize documentation by user journey: Getting Started, Guides, Reference, Advanced Topics. Keep hierarchy shallow (max 2-3 levels).
Use descriptive kebab-case filenames and group related docs into subdirectories.
Enable all markdownlint rules by default (default: true). Configure specific rules rather than disabling them.
Use consistent table formatting with aligned pipe characters and spacing.
Integrate markdownlint into CI/CD pipelines and pre-commit hooks to prevent regression.
Include alt text on all images, use descriptive link text (avoid 'click here'), and verify links work.
